conventions.colors_main: X in sheet — theme best expressed by the champion's kit, core items, team interaction, scaling
conventions.colors_off: O in sheet — theme influenced by item builds, playstyle, or matchup

color_codes.B: Black — power at a cost, sacrifice, conditions
color_codes.G: Green — synergy, curved spikes, item timings
color_codes.C: Colorless — dedicated theme, warps draft around it

champions.name: Aatrox
champions.ls_notes: mini games, if you fuck up his combos/resets you fuck up your power. B leads: the Q sweetspot conditions define his identity — aggression (R) is unlocked by executing them, not inherent.

champions.name: Aphelios
champions.ls_notes: dependent on the team composition. Weak against Twitch, Ziggs, Nilah, Seraphine, Draven — avoid picking into these or target them as counters if enemy has Aphelios. Works best with peel supports (Rakan, Thresh, Braum), not utility/poke supports (Senna, Naut, Xerath).

champions.name: Aurelion Sol
champions.ls_notes: G/U (reworked). G: stardust accumulation — grows permanently through the game, cosmic scaling identity. U: inevitability and map control, the breath of light as zone denial. R removed: he does not win through early aggression.

champions.name: Blitzcrank
champions.ls_notes: very red (LS). volatile: one hook changes the game or it doesn't. not a late game champion by itself. straightforward and linear — hook lands or it doesn't.

champions.name: Brand
champions.ls_notes: R/B. R: aggressive burst pressure, must win lane and snowball. B: passive stacks are condition-based — three procs for the blaze explosion. G removed: he does not scale with allies or have meaningful item timing synergy.

champions.name: Braum
champions.ls_notes: four-color champion. primary mode is W/R: go forward, protect, engage. G (synergy/items) and U (denial) only activated in specific contexts where he plays passive enabler.

champions.name: Camille
champions.ls_notes: Dimir Assassin ravnica

champions.name: Darius
champions.ls_notes: mono red (LS). closest champion in league to a pure single-color champion. just aggression, lane dominance, stacking bleed. one function.

champions.name: Diana
champions.ls_notes: ult interaction and q/w/e. whole kit has mini quests. items spikes. provides 0 value at even game state — needs a lead to function. R removed: aggression is conditional on the lead, not a baseline trait.

champions.name: Draven
champions.ls_notes: black red (LS). B leads: must collect axes, must win lane — conditions define everything. R is the output when the B conditions are met. Draven and Lucian come from the same school of thought; Draven hits harder when the conditions are met.

champions.name: Ezreal
champions.ls_notes: U is core: poke, safe play, distance control. can flip a switch to R aggression. W off: able to do everything, adapt to any comp. white is just a trait not core identity.

champions.name: Gangplank
champions.ls_notes: controls everything, scales/ramps up, still supportive. critplank, tankplank, can accelerate, can be a menace to kill. lots of synergy with botlane, item timings [IE, sterak, trinity, lvl 9/13]

champions.name: Garen
champions.ls_notes: Mono-R. No G identity — no ally synergy, no item timing complexity, no scaling in any G way. Pure linear aggression, same school as Darius and Olaf.

champions.name: Gragas
champions.ls_notes: can do everything (off-tank, glass cannon, pure tank). terrain shaping via E and R. little bit of blue (manipulation/terrain), little bit of red (early aggression switch). white is core.

champions.name: Graves
champions.ls_notes: lethality assassin relies on snowball, crit scales well, cleaver/ bruiser mix possibilities

champions.name: Janna
champions.ls_notes: W/G. W: resists aggression, binary disengage ult choice, counter-pick identity vs R comps — W vs R is the classic MTG counter. G: carry-enabling shield, accelerates ally power. U removed: control is secondary to her reactive W identity.

champions.name: Jarvan IV
champions.ls_notes: likes to be aggressive, strong early/mid, tapers off late game, bad if behind. can go glass cannon ad, offtank, pure tank, knockups, long reach, containment

champions.name: Kalista
champions.ls_notes: R/B. R: aggressive laning, must build a lead early. B: the Oathsworn bond is her defining mechanic — a condition at the core of her identity. G removed: off G/B was imprecise; B is main because the bond condition defines everything about how she plays.

champions.name: Karthus
champions.ls_notes: black black blue (LS). B leads: hates sharing magic damage — sacrifices team composition options for his power. U: terrain warping via passive/death, Requiem map control, slowing opponents. not R.

champions.name: Kayle
champions.ls_notes: Boros angel archetype. extremely white and red. W leads: structure, binary invulnerability ult, resists aggression. R secondary: damage payoff. G off: components of item timings and scaling but not the core identity.

champions.name: Kennen
champions.ls_notes: red green: aggressive AP all-in archetype, can't split push. item-completion dependent — needs completions to do anything. ult is a binary all-in. exhaust single-handedly neutralises his gameplan.

champions.name: Kha'Zix
champions.ls_notes: evolution quests are pure B — power unlocked through meeting conditions. 0 value at 0-0-0: needs a kill lead to snowball isolation windows. R removed from off: the aggression is gated behind the lead, not inherent. can manipulate teamfights in upgrades.

champions.name: Kindred
champions.ls_notes: white (LS) — protective, binary modes. W leads: marks system is a draft-choice condition, protective ultimate. G: item timing spikes, scales reasonably.

champions.name: LeBlanc
champions.ls_notes: R/U. R: must win early, assassination pressure. U: deception is core — clone, mimic, misdirection. The entire kit is built around making the enemy not know what is real.

champions.name: Leona
champions.ls_notes: R/W. R: all-in commit engager with no escape — she must go forward. W: binary supportive aspect, the shield and the protect-vs-engage choice. B removed: Sunlight passive is a minor condition, not her defining identity.

champions.name: Lucian
champions.ls_notes: black red. must win lane — same school of thought as Draven. R is the aggression; B is the condition (lane dominance must be met or he suffers greatly). flexibility comes at a cost.

champions.name: Lux
champions.ls_notes: green if mid full AP

champions.name: Nautilus
champions.ls_notes: U/W. U: Riptide zone control, CC chain suppression. W: supportive binary choices — hook engage or protect, the binary tanking decision. Not mono-U: his supportive protective identity is clearly W.

champions.name: Nunu & Willump
champions.ls_notes: G/U. G: the bond between Nunu and Willump — growing together, synergy-based power. U: snowball zone denial, Consume as map control. W removed: his identity is partnership growth, not binary choice support.

champions.name: Olaf
champions.ls_notes: one function (LS) — same archetype as Darius. pure linear aggression. runs at target, unstoppable. no meaningful off-color complexity.

champions.name: Rakan
champions.ls_notes: colorless when paried with Xayah

champions.name: Rek'sai
champions.ls_notes: Fundamentally flawed champion currently only breathing due to itemization and runes. R identity is intact but execution is item-dependent in a way that makes her fragile as a pick.

champions.name: Shyvana
champions.ls_notes: G/R. G: scales through objective stacking, item completions. R: dragon transformation is an aggressive empowerment — she shifts into an aggressive mode with her ult. Not mono-G.

champions.name: Sona
champions.ls_notes: Counter-pick ONLY — one of the strongest counter-picks in the game. Passive aura system makes her binary/reactive (W). Maximize basic abilities, not R. Counters: hard engage, dive assassins, poke comps, burst before she can heal. Constantly buffed, chronically underrated.

champions.name: Soraka
champions.ls_notes: G/W. G: healing = growth and restoration, enables allies to sustain through adversity. W: binary decisions about who to heal and when — the essential Choose One of support identity. U removed: her silence is denial-adjacent but not her defining color.

champions.name: Syndra
champions.ls_notes: R/U. R: aggressive burst pressure, must win lane. U: sphere placement is zone control — she denies space and dictates engagement angles. G removed: she does not scale with allies or have item timing synergy.

champions.name: Trundle
champions.ls_notes: support/pillar utility back to prime levels. fasting Senna opens flex potency. items now support the kit better than before.

champions.name: Tryndamere
champions.ls_notes: black champion (LS). excels by going around the opponent — dirty split-push value. B: comes at a cost, team must be sculpted to accommodate him and play 4v5. G: item timings, scaling into late game via farm.

champions.name: Twisted Fate
champions.ls_notes: W/U. W: Pick a Card is the quintessential binary Choose One mechanic. U: his Gate ult is pure U — inaction is action, you control the map without being there. Global presence with no combat required.

champions.name: Urgot
champions.ls_notes: viable jungler — funny combos, better items than before. totally viable top and very flexible into jungle; no one does it.

champions.name: Veigar
champions.ls_notes: blue and white (LS). U: stacks up inevitably, event horizon zone control, checkmates opponents by depleting options. W: binary answer — once online he just QR kills you, that's settle the wreckage. inaction is action.

champions.name: Viktor
champions.ls_notes: More niche than originally thought. Very dependent on items and factors beyond his control. U/W identity intact but execution is narrow.

champions.name: Xayah
champions.ls_notes: colorless when paried with Rakan

champions.name: Yasuo
champions.ls_notes: green red with black (LS). G: recipe-buy spikes (crit), scales over the top once conditions met. R: volatile, needs to get in. B off: conditions must align (knock-ups, team accommodation) for him to fully express; if they don't he falls apart.

champions.name: Zed
champions.ls_notes: Unfortunate victim of the assassin class. Needs items or ways to bypass honesty-weaknesses. Jungle was an interesting attempt at it. B — conditions define him.

champions.name: Nilah
champions.ls_notes: Grossly misunderstood and underutilized. W ability: 25% magic damage reduction + dodges autos for 2.5s, shares these with touched allies for 1.5s. G: synergy scaling, shares joy/power with allies. W: binary protective ability. C off: her pairings warp the draft around her — she is a comp-definer when played correctly.

champions.name: Rell
champions.ls_notes: R/W. R: all-in commit engage, magnetic pull forces confrontation. W: binary mount/dismount mechanic, the protective support aspect. G removed: no meaningful scaling or ally synergy in a G sense.

champions.name: Vex
champions.ls_notes: Strong as pure team comp counter and utility champ, not battlemage assassin. Fundamentally her class does not pair with modern itemization — treat as U/W denial/utility, not G.

champions.name: Zeri
champions.ls_notes: Team-comp dependent. Indirect buffs via champs she pairs with matter more than itemization. W/G — mobility and synergy-scaling identity.

champions.name: Renata Glasc
champions.ls_notes: One of the best champions in the game in terms of fundamental kit. Oddly almost does not care about items at all. B: Bailout (conditional second life — fight hard enough), Hostile Takeover ult (power at cost, forces enemies to turn). W: binary supportive kit choices. U off: zone control on E, deceptive threat range.

champions.name: Zaahen
champions.ls_notes: neo-Aatrox: base kit not strong enough to stand alone without item aid. reliant on what items are doing at a given point. such an honest champion with glaringly obvious weaknesses. B leads; R secondary like Aatrox.

notes: Color data: LS/Rübezahl spreadsheet (source='ls_sheet') + owner-tagged new releases (source='owner'). counter_tags present on owner-tagged entries; will be backfilled for ls_sheet entries via Phase 0 CLI.
